title: Some interesting details about Asuka
wikitext:
[]: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by bowen_shi » Sun Jun 17, 2018 8:38 pm SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Wed Jun 20, 2018 2:57 pm Yup, there's lots of subtle stuff like this in Eva! Honestly never payed attention to the episode 18 thing.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by The Cruel » Fri Nov 02, 2018 8:17 pm Nice analysis here. As much as Asuka hides her feelings for Shinji, to not appear weak in front of others, the more she doubts herself what Shinji means to her, when they both are hindered by their fears and therefore never got to know the essentials of love and living, thanks to their parents. And it only serves for selfish men and women who dared to be above Gods and Angels. To make them initiate 3I and live for the rest of their days as the last remaining humans, to never get to know about humanity existing again in aeons from evolving out of the primordial state, is the ultimate sacrifice to save what's left in the face of the universe.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Sun Nov 11, 2018 10:58 pm That is the best detailed reasoning behind the argument that Asuka loves Shinji, or at the very least cared deeply for him. I never noticed that Asuka saw Shinji happy in her mind, and you are right to point out that he was not always a happy camper, and I don't ever recall him smiling around Asuka, save for one or two times. For her to think of that memory above all others about Shinji considering how she outwardly treats him, she must love him.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Nov 13, 2018 3:26 pm Late to the party, but yeah, these are great details. Asuka would never admit it, but she's very concerned for Shinji.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by PenPen4life » Mon Jan 14, 2019 3:22 am To make them initiate 3I and live for the rest of their days as the last remaining humans, to never get to know about humanity existing again in aeons from evolving out of the primordial state, is the ultimate sacrifice to save what's left in the face of the universe.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by The Cruel » Mon Jan 14, 2019 6:56 am ^ You can’t be sure that Shinji and Asuka ‘ll still be alive to witness humanity to come back. And even if it takes a strong will for humans to come back, it doesn‘t mean that this will happen so easly and in any moment. Time and Space are too fucked at this point. The last time all of life on earth could evolve out from the primodial state into a stable state before 2I and 3I took aeons. Humanity will never get to know that Shinji and Asuka had ever existed. It‘s the same as them being buried alive. Shinji and Asuka are death stranded.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jan 14, 2019 8:14 am The Cruel wrote: And even if it takes a strong will for humans to come back, it doesn‘t mean that this will happen so easily and in any moment.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by The Cruel » Mon Jan 14, 2019 9:44 am pwhodges wrote: But nor is there any specific justification for your extremely pessimistic view.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by PenPen4life » Mon Jan 14, 2019 12:26 pm The Cruel wrote: ^ You can’t be sure that Shinji and Asuka ‘ll still be alive to witness humanity to come back.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 14, 2019 1:12 pm And people said that I was being pessimistic, back in the day ...

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by PenPen4life » Mon Jan 14, 2019 2:22 pm Mr. Tines wrote: And people said that I was being pessimistic, back in the day ...

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by ACGT-Samael » Mon Jan 14, 2019 3:27 pm PenPen4life wrote: How is this misconception still going on.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by dzzthink » Sun Aug 23, 2020 4:31 pm Hi everyone, I just want to add to this post, because there is an amazing amount of detail in the scene where Asuka and Shinji kiss. I didn't make this discovery myself but it was posted as a comment by Jonathan Wang on the YouTube video of the scene https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kME7NJrxhow . He notes that: "you can actually see Asuka get subtly more irritated as the kiss goes on due to Shinji's inaction. At 0:50 or so in addition to Shinji's face color change, her eyebrows are also subtly shifted to one that makes her look annoyed". Such a tiny transition really says a lot in the context of the series, but you really have to look closely for this one!

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by ShinjiStranglesAsuka » Sun Aug 23, 2020 11:05 pm dzzthink wrote: snip

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Mon Aug 24, 2020 5:14 pm It's a shame this sort of detail is rarely reproduced in dubs. I remember the ADV dub had a bunch of "naughty naughty" moans in that scene and it completely breaks things. In EoE, too, Miyamura gave Asuka an exasperated tone confronting Shinji that just gives a *hint* of heartbreak and dissapointment, but in the dub she was just mad and that's it.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Aug 24, 2020 9:18 pm Why would a dub need to reproduce a purely visual detail?

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Aug 25, 2020 1:46 am Never ever noticed the eyebrow shift. And I've watched the show an insane amount of times.

[]: Re: Some interesting details about Asuka             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Aug 25, 2020 11:37 am Reichu wrote: Why would a dub need to reproduce a purely visual detail?
